title_header: Tower of God, Episode 10: 2F Lero Ro's Test

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Evankhell's Mothership. The Second Test Point.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Wow, they all seem powerful and vicious.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Everyone looks formidable. They are survivors of the first test, indeed.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Now it feels like the real test has begun! I'm excited!

dialogue.speaker: SHIBISU
dialogue.line: What do you think, Hatz? Aren't you excited to see those powerful guys? Is your fighting spirit rising?

dialogue.speaker: HATZ
dialogue.line: Because of your chattering mouth, my sword arm wants to strike.

dialogue.speaker: SHIBISU
dialogue.line: I-is that so? Well then, Anaak, how do you feel? Aren't you excited or--

dialogue.speaker: ANAAK
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, excited. I'm very excited to be on a team with you, you useless blabbermouth.

dialogue.speaker: SHIBISU
dialogue.line: Ah, okay. You-- I don't think you know me well, but I am really strong! I've learned deadly martial arts!

dialogue.speaker: ANAAK
dialogue.line: Don't lie, you know.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Martial arts is a secret form of combat passed down from the Tower--

dialogue.speaker: ANAAK
dialogue.line: Just shut up!

dialogue.speaker: ANAAK
dialogue.line: I-I've never been to a place with such a crowd, Knight. I'm so scared.

dialogue.speaker: BAM
dialogue.line: Don't worry, Lady Anaak. I will always be your shield.

dialogue.speaker: KHUN
dialogue.line: Oh my, how sweet. Love's blossoming here.

dialogue.speaker: KHUN
dialogue.line: Hey, I haven't been to a place with this many people either. Maybe you're bad at socializing.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Whatever.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Oh, it's that red-haired guy. How did he pass the team test?!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Oh, him? He attacked everyone he met. And killed all of them there? Mercilessly? He's vicious!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: How could anyone team up with him? I don't get it.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Well, his teammates don't look ordinary either.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Gosh, why does he keep thumping?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Are you sure we can trust him?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Haha, I guarantee he's strong.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Amazing, this many regulars passed the test.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Well, it looks like there are more than 100. They are the chosen ones, for sure. There's no doubt they passed the test.

dialogue.speaker: KHUN
dialogue.line: Be careful, Bam. Some of them have unusual powers. Some are cheap and dirty players.

dialogue.speaker: KHUN
dialogue.line: The chosen regulars who came from the Residential Area must have at least one extraordinary talent. Don't underestimate any of them.

dialogue.speaker: RAK
dialogue.line: All the regulars here are ferocious enemies!

dialogue.speaker: RAK
dialogue.line: Well, I'm sure we're the strongest team! Because our leader is the awesome and powerful Mr. Crocodile!

dialogue.speaker: RAK
dialogue.line: Haha! Who's what?! You tricky turtles! Get your hands off of me! Bug off!

dialogue.speaker: KHUN
dialogue.line: Rak! How can you do that to a teammate?

dialogue.speaker: RAK
dialogue.line: You're not my teammate! You jerk!

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Story by Slave. In. Utero. Tower of God. 2F Lero Ro's Test.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Finally! I gotcha! I will kill you!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Look what you did to my arm! How are you going to make up for this?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Kuk? I don't know. Maybe you can buy a stick and attach it to your arm.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: What? You! Jerk!

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: Stop right there, pals. I'm sorry to see your arm like that, but fighting is prohibited during the break. And you got hurt during the test, so it was justified.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Justified? Kuk? I was ambushed! I'm not so weak as to be beaten by this guy!

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: What I mean is-- I'm sure you would've been surely beaten even if it wasn't an ambush. You're just weak.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: You're really asking for it, aren't you?! Well, you got it! Bring it on! Let's fight! I don't care about the test anymore!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: They seem to be fighting during the break, Mr. Khun. Shouldn't we stop them?

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: Leave 'em. They will be stopped soon. Did you not understand? No fighting during break time.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: There you go. You didn't take my words seriously, did you, regulars?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Who's this guy? He looks like a cricket!

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: Do you wanna die, too?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: S-stop! I think he's--!!

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: I'm the Test Administrator, Lero Ro.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Test Administrator? Then you are a Ranker?

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: Yes, I'm a Ranker of the Tower.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Real Ranker, for real?!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: D-damn it! I'm only backing down because the Ranker's here!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: But remember! I'll be the one to kill you!

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: Hmph. Well, well.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: Isn't this a feisty crowd?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Wow, he's good. He didn't say much, but he stopped the fight.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Yeah, of course. He's a Ranker and the Test Administrator.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: A Ranker? What? By the way, what's a 'Ranker'?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: You don't even know about Rankers?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Rankers are people who've reached the top of the Tower. They are assigned a ranking every season in the Tower, and that's why they are called Rankers.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: To-- to the top of the Tower?!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Yeah, they're amazing. Only one or two out of thousands of regulars get to be Rankers. Quite frankly, they are monsters. They've got crazy power.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Reached the top of the Tower. Those two, they were amazing people.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: My test, regulars! Please focus!

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: I will explain the next round. Before that, to give you some brief information about myself, I'm your Test Administrator, Ranker Lero Ro.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: Nice to meet you.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: Now that you've had some time to rest, let's move on to the next test!

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: But before that, there will be a little pretest!

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: FYI! Those who don't pass this pretest cannot take the next test!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: ?! What? That means it is another elimination test! What is he testing?

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: Ha ha! Don't worry! It is a really simple pretest! Relax, all right, let's begin, the pretest. Okay.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: This way and that way, here and there, and everywhere, and boom!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: What's this?! Darn it! Is this Shinsu?

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: Yep, I created a barrier of Shinsu. I compressed the Shinsu to make a barrier that pushed you all out.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: As you well know, Shinsu is everywhere in the Tower, and it plays an important role because it allows us to breathe and provides water.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: However, Shinsu can sometimes be a powerful murder weapon depending on how it's used.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: Shinsu is so strong that it can break an arm, so all the contestants in the Tower use Shinsu to fight.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: However, there are people who show adverse reactions to Shinsu. In other words, those who can't deal well with the Shinsu are not appropriate candidates to be in the Tower.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: The people who can pass through this veil barrier don't have adverse reactions to Shinsu, at least.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: The teams that pass through the barrier pass the test as well, and those who don't, fail.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: All right then, everyone, please come through.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Wait! Did you say team?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: What if one of your teammates can't pass through the barrier?

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: Isn't it obvious? That team fails.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: That's absurd! Doesn't make sense!

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: Absurd? What is?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Yeah! We joined teams last minute! It's not fair if I'm eliminated because of a teammate! That's just bad luck!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: It's rubbish! Is this a lottery?

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: Hey, do you know what is the most important to move up the Tower?

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: It's luck. The luck of having a strong body, the luck of being smart, the luck of being quick, the luck of escaping from death.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: The luck of saving a teammate. The only reason you got this far is because you were lucky.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: And now you say it's absurd to test luck?

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: Stop your jabbering and just take the test, you regulars.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Damn it, he's saying whatever he wants, which means this has nothing to do with fairness.

dialogue.speaker: KHUN
dialogue.line: Well, actually, the concentration of the Shinsu in that barrier doesn't look that high. Since he's been hunting in the Shinsu, he'll easily pass.

dialogue.speaker: KHUN
dialogue.line: The problem is Bam.

dialogue.speaker: BAM
dialogue.line: Bam? I'm sorry, Mr. Lero Ro.

dialogue.speaker: BAM
dialogue.line: I... I don't know why, but I wasn't pushed out.

dialogue.speaker: BAM
dialogue.line: I... To take the test, I should probably go out and come back in, right?

dialogue.speaker: BAM
dialogue.line: ?! Ah, ha ha ha, I... It's... I-I'm sorry! I will go back out! It's a test after all!

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: No, Mr. Bam, you're in.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: I think I made a mistake, but, ha ha, that is also part of your luck.

dialogue.speaker: LERO RO
dialogue.line: You passed, Mr. Bam.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: What?! Damn it! That stupid luck thing!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: He's got all the luck, huh?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: W-what? What's going on? Boo! He couldn't possibly have made a mistake! Damn! So the world is all about luck, huh?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Crocodile and I were right next to him but we were pushed out! Like a lottery!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: You will never walk alone, you will never walk alone.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: It's not a mistake! He just wasn't pushed out!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: What is this? Who is he?!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: A monster has arrived.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Tower of God. To be continued.

summary: The regulars gather for the second test, where Lero Ro, the Test Administrator and a Ranker, intervenes in a fight and introduces a pretest involving a Shinsu barrier. He explains that teams will fail if any member cannot pass, emphasizing the role of luck in the Tower. Surprisingly, Bam passes the Shinsu barrier effortlessly, causing confusion and frustration among the other participants.
